accept: act create <title> --blocked-by <id> [--blocked-by <id>...] accepts one or more dep targets and writes the create + dep-add ops in a single auto-commit.
On any failure after create succeeds, the partial state is rolled back (op file unstaged) so the bug never exists with no edge.
MCP composed tool act_file_blocker accepts {title, blocked_by, ...create-flags} and is one tool call.
act_file_blocker flips the blocking issue to status=blocked iff a --block-parent flag is set; default is to file-and-link without modifying the parent.
Surface-gap-analysis Workflows A and C are reduced from 2 calls to 1.
payload.description: Workflows A (file a bug after a failed test) and C (file a follow-up while implementing X) both require: create issue, add blocks edge. Today this is two non-atomic calls. If the second fails, an orphaned bug exists with no edge to its blocker. The fix is a composed primitive. Originally specified at docs/issues/act-g003-gap.md.
payload.title: act create --blocked-by and composed act_file_blocker MCP tool
